_meta.keyKnowledge: We explore how music elements communicate meaning across cultures, times and places, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander music.

_source.w: We explore how music elements communicate meaning across cultures, times and places, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander music.
y: Understanding how music works across cultures deepens our appreciation and broadens our perspective.
Analysing music helps us understand why certain sounds affect us the way they do.
Exploring diverse music traditions reveals the universal and unique qualities of music.
eg: We listen to music with different forms — verse-chorus, binary, theme and variations — and describe what we hear.
We identify the roles of instruments and voices in music from different traditions.
We compare how different cultures use rhythm, melody and texture in their music.
We explore how music has evolved within specific cultural traditions over time.
We analyse how elements like tempo, dynamics and timbre create mood and meaning.
We investigate how Australian music draws on diverse cultural influences.
